name: A-MEM
tagline: Agentic memory that lets LLM agents organize and link their notes Zettelkasten-style
seoTitle: A-MEM: Agentic Memory for LLM Agents
metaDescription: A-MEM is a Python memory system for LLM agents that dynamically organizes notes, links related memories with ChromaDB, and evolves them over time.
overview: A-MEM (Agentic Memory) is a research memory system for LLM agents that stores each piece of information as a structured note and links related notes into an evolving network, inspired by the Zettelkasten method. Instead of plain storage and retrieval, it generates tags, context, and keywords for new memories and connects them to existing ones.

It is aimed at developers and researchers building agents that need to reuse past experiences rather than start fresh on every task. It fits the memory-frameworks category by sitting between your agent and a vector store, handling organization, linking, and evolution on top of basic save and search.

A-MEM uses ChromaDB for vector storage and semantic search, and supports OpenAI or local Ollama models as the LLM backend. Note that this repository is meant for building agents; the authors point to a separate repo to reproduce the paper's published results.
features: Dynamic memory organization based on Zettelkasten principles, building an interconnected note network
Semantic indexing, linking, and similarity search backed by ChromaDB vector storage
Automatic note generation with structured attributes: tags, context, and extracted keywords
Continuous memory evolution that updates metadata and creates links when memories are added or changed
Pluggable LLM backends, supporting OpenAI (GPT-4, GPT-3.5) and Ollama for local deployment
Standard CRUD operations: add, read, search, update, and delete notes by ID

gettingStarted.intro: A-MEM is installed from source. Clone the repo, install the package into a virtual environment, then initialize the memory system in Python.

steps.heading: Clone the repository
steps.body: Get the source from GitHub and move into the project directory.

steps.heading: Create a virtual environment and install
steps.body: A virtual environment is recommended. Install the package with pip (use -e for editable/development mode).
steps.code:
python -m venv .venv
source .venv/bin/activate  # On Windows, use: .venv\Scripts\activate
pip install .

steps.heading: Initialize the memory system and add a note
steps.body: Create an AgenticMemorySystem with an embedding model and an LLM backend, then add and search memories.
steps.code:
from agentic_memory.memory_system import AgenticMemorySystem

memory_system = AgenticMemorySystem(
    model_name='all-MiniLM-L6-v2',  # Embedding model for ChromaDB
    llm_backend="openai",           # LLM backend (openai/ollama)
    llm_model="gpt-4o-mini"         # LLM model name
)

memory_id = memory_system.add_note("Deep learning neural networks")
results = memory_system.search_agentic("neural networks", k=5)
for result in results:
    print(result['id'], result['content'])

useCases: Give an LLM agent long-term memory so it can reuse past tasks and conversations instead of starting fresh each time
Build a self-organizing knowledge base where related notes are automatically tagged and linked
Run agent memory locally with Ollama models and ChromaDB when you want to avoid sending data to a hosted API
Experiment with or extend the A-MEM research framework for studies on memory organization in LLM agents
